concepts.name: Doubly Linked List
concepts.short_definition: A linked list where each node has pointers to both the next and previous nodes.

concepts.name: Node
concepts.short_definition: A fundamental building block of a linked list, containing data and pointers.

concepts.name: Data Field
concepts.short_definition: The part of a node that stores the actual information.

concepts.name: Next Pointer
concepts.short_definition: A pointer within a node that references the subsequent node in the list.

concepts.name: Previous Pointer
concepts.short_definition: A pointer within a node that references the preceding node in the list.

concepts.name: Head Pointer
concepts.short_definition: A pointer to the first node in the doubly linked list.

concepts.name: Tail Pointer
concepts.short_definition: A pointer to the last node in the doubly linked list.

concepts.name: Null Pointer
concepts.short_definition: A pointer that does not point to any valid memory location, often indicating the end of a list or an empty field.

concepts.name: Empty List
concepts.short_definition: A doubly linked list that contains no nodes.

concepts.name: Insertion
concepts.short_definition: The process of adding a new node to the doubly linked list.

concepts.name: Insertion at Beginning
concepts.short_definition: Adding a new node at the start of the doubly linked list.

concepts.name: Insertion at End
concepts.short_definition: Adding a new node at the end of the doubly linked list.

concepts.name: Insertion in Middle
concepts.short_definition: Adding a new node at a specific position within the doubly linked list.

concepts.name: Deletion
concepts.short_definition: The process of removing a node from the doubly linked list.

concepts.name: Deletion from Beginning
concepts.short_definition: Removing the first node from the doubly linked list.

concepts.name: Deletion from End
concepts.short_definition: Removing the last node from the doubly linked list.

concepts.name: Deletion from Middle
concepts.short_definition: Removing a node at a specific position or with a specific value from the doubly linked list.

concepts.name: Traversal
concepts.short_definition: Visiting each node in the doubly linked list in sequence.

concepts.name: Forward Traversal
concepts.short_definition: Traversing the list from the head to the tail using the 'next' pointers.

concepts.name: Backward Traversal
concepts.short_definition: Traversing the list from the tail to the head using the 'previous' pointers.

concepts.name: Search
concepts.short_definition: Locating a node with a specific value within the doubly linked list.

concepts.name: Update
concepts.short_definition: Changing the data field of an existing node in the doubly linked list.

concepts.name: Memory Allocation
concepts.short_definition: The process of reserving memory for a new node when it's created.

concepts.name: Memory Deallocation
concepts.short_definition: The process of releasing memory occupied by a deleted node.

concepts.name: Circular Doubly Linked List
concepts.short_definition: A doubly linked list where the last node's 'next' pointer points to the first node, and the first node's 'previous' pointer points to the last node.

concepts.name: Pointer Manipulation
concepts.short_definition: The act of changing the 'next' and 'previous' pointers to modify the list structure.

concepts.name: Edge Cases
concepts.short_definition: Unusual or boundary conditions that need to be handled correctly in list operations (e.g., empty list, single-node list).

concepts.name: Time Complexity
concepts.short_definition: A measure of the efficiency of an algorithm in terms of the input size, often expressed using Big O notation.

concepts.name: Space Complexity
concepts.short_definition: A measure of the amount of memory an algorithm uses in relation to the input size.

concepts.name: Doubly Linked List vs Singly Linked List
concepts.short_definition: Comparing the advantages and disadvantages of doubly linked lists versus singly linked lists.

concepts.name: Doubly Linked List vs Array
concepts.short_definition: Comparing the performance and use cases of doubly linked lists against arrays.

concepts.name: Doubly Linked List Implementation
concepts.short_definition: Writing the actual code to create and manipulate a doubly linked list.

concepts.name: Doubly Linked List Operations
concepts.short_definition: The set of standard procedures performed on a doubly linked list (e.g., insert, delete, traverse).

concepts.name: Doubly Linked List Applications
concepts.short_definition: Real-world scenarios and data structures where doubly linked lists are effectively used.

concepts.name: Doubly Linked List Advantages
concepts.short_definition: The benefits of using a doubly linked list over other data structures.

concepts.name: Doubly Linked List Disadvantages
concepts.short_definition: The drawbacks and limitations of using a doubly linked list.

concepts.name: Doubly Linked List Memory Overhead
concepts.short_definition: The extra memory required by a doubly linked list due to the additional 'previous' pointer.

concepts.name: Doubly Linked List Reversal
concepts.short_definition: The process of reversing the order of nodes in a doubly linked list.

concepts.name: Doubly Linked List Merging
concepts.short_definition: Combining two doubly linked lists into a single list.

concepts.name: Doubly Linked List Sorting
concepts.short_definition: Arranging the nodes of a doubly linked list in a specific order.
